preamble: Palatal glides can vocalize into high front vowels, typically occurring in word-final positions or between consonants.

examples.note: Final glide /j/ historical outcomes often vocalize.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
